serving.natural_language_portion: One standard cup of brewed Ceylon black tea, approximately 240 ml, prepared by steeping Ceylon black tea leaves in hot water without milk or sugar.

nutrient_source.title: Tea, black, brewed, prepared with tap water (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy, FDC ID 1104262)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA SR Legacy entry for brewed black tea prepared with tap water is directly applicable to plain brewed Ceylon black tea without milk or sugar. Ceylon black tea is a variety of Camellia sinensis; nutrient profile of brewed black tea is consistent across varieties at the plain-brewed level.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of plain brewed black tea; effectively zero GI for non-caloric beverages
gi_source.source_match_notes: Plain brewed black tea contains negligible carbohydrates (0.3 g per 100 g) and no glycemic carbohydrates. The University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed literature confirm that plain unsweetened black tea has an effectively zero glycemic response. glycemic_index set to 1 (the lowest valid positive sentinel value) per Project Veritas convention for zero-GI beverages. gi_category is low. glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0 as carbohydrate content per serving is below the threshold for meaningful glycemic load calculation.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium content (3 mg per 100 g); no added salt
hypertension.guidance: Plain brewed Ceylon black tea is appropriate for individuals with hypertension due to its negligible sodium content; however, patients should avoid adding salt or high-sodium accompaniments, and moderate caffeine intake if sensitive to blood pressure elevation from caffeine.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Negligible carbohydrate content and effectively zero glycemic index
type2_diabetes.guidance: Plain unsweetened Ceylon black tea has an effectively zero glycemic index and negligible carbohydrate content (0.3 g per 100 g), making it a suitable beverage choice for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; patients should avoid adding sugar or sweetened condensed milk, which would substantially raise the glycemic load.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Zero saturated fat, zero cholesterol, and presence of tea polyphenols (theaflavins, thearubigins)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Plain brewed Ceylon black tea contains no saturated fat or cholesterol and provides tea polyphenols that have been associated with modest cardioprotective effects in observational studies; it is a heart-friendly beverage choice when consumed without added sugar or full-fat milk.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Ceylon Black Tea is a variety of black tea produced from the Camellia sinensis plant, grown in the highlands of Sri Lanka (formerly Ceylon). It is one of the world's most widely consumed teas, known for its bold, brisk, and slightly astringent flavour with a bright amber-to-deep-red liquor. Ceylon black tea is fully oxidised during processing, which distinguishes it from green or oolong teas and gives it its characteristic dark colour and robust taste. In Singapore and across Southeast Asia, Ceylon black tea is commonly served hot as a plain brewed tea, or as the base for teh tarik (pulled milk tea) and other milk tea preparations. When served plain without milk or sugar, brewed Ceylon black tea is essentially calorie-free, containing only approximately 1 kcal per 100 g, with negligible protein, fat, and carbohydrate. It provides small amounts of potassium (37 mg per 100 g), riboflavin, folate, and trace minerals. The tea contains caffeine (approximately 20–40 mg per 240 ml cup) and bioactive polyphenols including theaflavins and thearubigins, which are formed during oxidation and have been studied for antioxidant and potential cardioprotective properties. Its glycemic index is effectively zero, making it suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or metabolic conditions, provided it is consumed without added sugar. The sodium content is negligible (3 mg per 100 g), making it appropriate for individuals with hypertension. Ceylon black tea is vegan, gluten-free, dairy-free, halal, and kosher when prepared plain.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 1104262) for brewed black tea prepared with tap water, which is the standard reference for plain brewed black tea. Ceylon black tea is a Camellia sinensis variety and its brewed nutrient profile at the plain-brewed level is consistent with the USDA entry. GI is well-established in the literature as effectively zero for plain unsweetened black tea.
notes: Plain brewed Ceylon black tea without milk or sugar. energy_kcal is 1.0 kcal per 100 g as confirmed by USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 1104262); this is naturally near-zero for a brewed tea beverage. protein_g is naturally zero as brewed black tea contains no protein detectable above reporting thresholds per USDA FoodData Central. fat_g is naturally zero as brewed black tea contains no fat per USDA FoodData Central; confirmed zero. saturated_fat_g is naturally zero; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. sugar_g is naturally zero for plain unsweetened brewed black tea; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. dietary_fibre_g is naturally zero for brewed tea (fibre remains in the leaf, not the infusion); confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. cholesterol_mg is naturally zero; confirmed zero. glycemic_index set to 1 (effectively zero) as this beverage contains no glycemic carbohydrates; gi_category low per Project Veritas convention for zero-GI beverages. glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0 as carbohydrate content per serving is negligible. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, and trans_fat_g are 0.0 as confirmed by USDA FoodData Central for brewed black tea. potassium_mg value of 37.0 mg per 100 g is from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy. folate_mcg_dfe value of 5.0 mcg per 100 g is from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy. riboflavin_mg value of 0.03 mg per 100 g is from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy. iron_mg value of 0.02 mg per 100 g is from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy. zinc_mg value of 0.02 mg per 100 g is from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy. phosphorus_mg value of 1.0 mg per 100 g is from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy. magnesium_mg value of 3.0 mg per 100 g is from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy. water_g value of 99.7 g per 100 g is from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy. Cuisine tag in input is listed as Chinese, which reflects the serving context at Happy Cup - Funan (a Chinese-style tea café in Singapore); region_of_origin is correctly set to Sri Lanka as the origin of Ceylon tea. Multilingual names provided for Singaporean context (English, Chinese Simplified, Chinese Traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) as the food is served in Singapore. field survey data source URL retained as provenance of the seed record only; nutrient data sourced from USDA FoodData Central as the approved primary source.
